item.title: 156 citizens demand release of Emi, Mamun
item.description: One hundred and fifty-six citizens, including academics, poets, writers, artistes, and journalists on Tuesday demanded unconditional release of two youths, who were arrested after playing the historic March 7, 1971 speech of the country’s founding president......

item.title: Jamaat amir seeks his adviser be given minister status at foreign ministry
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir and also leader of the opposition in Jatiya Sangsad, Shafiqur Rahman, in an unusual move sought that his foreign affairs adviser Professor Mohammad Mahmudul Hasan be appointed at the foreign affairs ministry as an adviser or.......

item.title: Police control established in Jungle Salimpur, says Ctg DIG
item.description: Twenty-two people were arrested and three cases were filed over the recovery of a large quantity of arms, ammunition, and explosives during a joint force operation in the remote hilly areas of Jungle Salimpur in Sitakunda upazila of Chattogram....

item.title: Family card funds being disbursed through Nagad
item.description: Mobile financial service Nagad is playing a significant role in the government’s newly launched Family Card programme aimed at economically empowering women heads of families and boosting their confidence, said a press release....

item.title: Govt mulls revoking bachelor’s degree requirement
item.description: The education ministry is planning to relax or remove soon the current educational requirement for the chairmen of the governing bodies of the non-government educational institutions to have at least a bachelor’s degree amid a fear of including politicians in these bodies....

item.title: Illumination goes on defying directives
item.description: The government directive to refrain from excessive decorative lighting at shopping malls on the occasion of Eid has largely been ignored as competing shopping malls do not want to fall behind others in drawing the attention of customers....

item.title: Women and the crisis of democracy
item.description: THE metaphor of the ‘engine seat’ in a local bus, once joked about by my late professor during my undergraduate years, remains the most stinging and accurate description of how women are integrated into the Bangladeshi political and social fabric. These seats, positioned directly above the vibrating, overheated engine, are technically ‘reserved’ for women. Yet......

item.title: The duty to rebuild
item.description: There are moments in a nation’s life when victory does not feel like triumph at all. It feels like an inventory. One counts the broken things. The bent institutions. The exhausted treasury. The roads that still exist but no longer promise movement. The school rooms in which children are present but learning is absent. The offices where authority survived but purpose did......

item.title: Bangladesh sails for Gaza
item.description: I HAVE stood on a deck headed towards Gaza. I know what that feels like — the sea ahead of you, and the knowledge, sitting heavy in your chest, that on the other side people are being killed while the world carefully arranges its indifference. The brutality of the genocide, the horror of forced starvation. the hypocrisy of western media, were plain for the world to see......

item.title: Middle East conflict and global economy
item.description: THERE is something almost ritualistic about the moment when oil prices cross the psychologically powerful threshold of $100 per barrel. Markets shudder. Politicians panic. Economists dust off old vocabulary — shock, disruption, stagflation. The global economy, so modern in appearance yet so primitive in its dependence on fossil fuel, begins to wobble......

item.title: Teacher shortage, poor pay, other failures fuel tuition trap
item.description: THE education system is caught in a paradox where a booming coaching industry quietly fills the gaps. Academics and guardians report that teachers across the spectrum often supplement their income by providing private tuition, in some cases even pressuring students to attend. Coaching centres, exploiting the absence of an effective regulatory mechanism......

item.title: Building empathetic society must be priority
item.description: GROWING incidents of public brutality signal the troubling normalisation of violence and a gradual erosion of empathy. In three incidents on March 9, three people were killed in public view. A young man was beaten to death over a fuel dispute at a filling station in Jhenaidah, sparking protests, bus arson and a five-hour highway blockade while the police arrested three......

item.title: Home ministry reviews 4,000 SI posts creation proposal
item.description: The ministry of home affairs on Tuesday held a meeting to review the proposal of creating 4,000 sub-inspector posts to strengthen the capacity of Bangladesh Police in maintaining law and order across the country....

item.title: Lingering war to affect economy: commerce minister
item.description: Commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir on Tuesday said that if the United States and Israel’s war on Iran and its retaliation across the Gulf last long, it would have a negative impact on the sea route and the importing of goods....

item.title: One more Jamaat leader dies after 10 days
item.description: A leader of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami, who was critically injured in a clash between the activists of Jamaat and the Bangladesh Nationalist Party in Jibannagar upazila of Chuadanga 10 days ago, died on Tuesday while undergoing treatment at a hospital in Dhaka....Geographic Reference

item.title: CAAB stresses human resource dev for aviation safety
item.description: Civil Aviation Academy has stressed prioritising human resource development to ensure safety and efficiency in the country’s aviation sector, highlighting the importance of competency-based training aligned with international standards....

item.title: Mother withdraws complaint over assault on DU student
item.description: Parveen Akter, mother of Dhaka University student Rahid Khan Pavel who was allegedly beaten up on campus on Monday night, withdrew the complaint she had filed with the Shahbagh Police Station....

item.title: Extremists becoming active again: RAB
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion on Tuesday said that extremists were trying to become active again and that some who had surrendered in the past had resumed criminal activities after the 2024 mass uprising that ousted the Awami League regime from power....

item.title: Journalists face restrictions, detention covering ME war
item.description: Journalists covering the war in the Middle East are facing increasing restrictions and censorship imposed by governments and armed groups, with reporters being stopped and questioned or even detained, a survey of AFP bureau chiefs from the region showed....

item.title: Russia kills four in east Ukraine
item.description: Russian strikes killed four people and wounded more than a dozen others in the embattled eastern Ukrainian town of Sloviansk, the regional authorities announced Tuesday....

item.title: Student dies after falling from school rooftop
item.description: A class IX student of Shaheed Academic School and College in Tangail died on Tuesday after falling from the rooftop of the six-storey school building....

item.title: Control room opened for Middle East workers
item.description: The ministry of expatriates’ welfare and overseas employment opened a control room to ensure the welfare and safety of Bangladeshi expatriate workers amid the ongoing war situation in the Middle East....

item.title: Robbers loot house in broad daylight in city
item.description: A group of five to six suspected robbers looted 10-12 bharis of gold and Tk 20,000 to 25,000 from a house in the capital’s South Manipur area at Mirpur-2 in the capital Dhaka on Tuesday afternoon....

item.title: Coast Guard arrests ‘robber’ in Sundarban
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested a member of the robber group named ‘Dayal Bahini’ along with weapons and ammunition in an operation in the Sundarban on Tuesday....

item.title: Extreme heat cuts productivity
item.description: Extreme heat is increasingly hampering productivity in Bangladesh, with people engaged in outdoor activities now exposed to dangerous heat conditions for about 2,500 hours a year — up from about 2,180 hours during the 20th century, according to a new study....

item.title: GAUAA draft constitution handed over to VC
item.description: The Alumni Association of Gazipur Agricultural University on Tuesday handed over its draft constitution to the vice-chancellor of the university Professor GKM Mustafizur Rahman, marking a step toward building a strong and well-connected alumni network for the university....

item.title: BMU DAB leaders congratulate new VC
item.description: The BMU unit leaders of the Doctors’ Association of Bangladesh congratulated the newly appointed vice-chancellor of Bangladesh Medical University Professor Dr AFM Siddiqui on Tuesday....

item.title: SKOP demands wages, allowance before Eid
item.description: Leaders of the Sramik Karmachari Oikya Parishad, at a protest rally in Dhaka on Tuesday, called on the government and factory owners to pay the wages and festival allowances of the workers before Eid-ul-Fitr....Maps
